title: Relationships and fitting in — the Kaisa investment question
html:
Relationships and fitting in — the Kaisa investment question

Source: 2022-10-15 Google Doc opened mid-Human-Environment-Geography lecture. Yahir interrogates whether his interest in Kaisa is real attraction or peer-pressure conformity to the OSU friend group's coupled-up status quo.

TL;DR

  • Trigger — the "mismatched socks" / 4/20 trolling joke that "if you don't have a pair you should be tossed out" surfaces an invisible pressure to be partnered.
  • The investment frame — Yahir reframes a relationship as an investment of time/energy "for it to be reciprocated into something beautiful" — but flags the danger of starting from that frame.
  • The butterfly question — "can I be with someone if I don't get a weird feeling butterfly type feeling towards them" — he provisionally calls this a fallacy ("that will grow").
  • Self-correction — "Fuck everyone else honestly. Fitting in, you are the front line, actually going to be remembered."
  • Fantasy of the daily — cooking for someone, "random spot up dates," sharing struggles and wins — domestic-attachment imagery that recurs years later in the Emily arc.

Pattern observed

This is a 2022 OSU-era proto-version of the relationship-audit pattern that recurs through the 2025 Austin Emily reevaluation and the 2026 alignment audit — Yahir keeps catching himself either fitting-in-by-coupling or weighing real attachment against distance / proximity as power tradeoffs. The Kaisa moment is the first cataloged instance of "am I doing this because the fellas have it" as an explicit self-audit prompt.

The note also reaffirms a Yahir-marker: he treats relationships through the same systems-thinking lens as everything else — investment, ROI, "a fallacy because that will grow" — see asking the right question and regret minimization framework.

Open questions

  • Did Yahir actually pursue Kaisa? She doesn't recur in any later cataloged note (queue first-name-only to Potential Entities).
  • How does this 2022 framing compare to the 2026 Emily circle deliberation?
